Off Calendar Feb-26-2016 Inactivate Attorney

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (Or,In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues)
Off Calendar Feb-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JAMES P. GOSLEE, ESQ. of Cohen Placitella & Roth PC, Tel No. 215-567-3500 appearing for Plaintiff AIG Speciality Insurance Company;
Attys. SAMUEL H. RUBY, ESQ. and HEATHER J. ZACHARIA, ESQ. of Bullivant Houser Bailey PC, Tel No. 415-352-2700 appearing for Defendant Interstate Fire & Casualty Company.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT INTERSTATE FIRE & CASUALTY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Based on the undisputed facts, Interstate is entitled to judgment in its favor on all three claims asserted by AIG. The undisputed facts show that: 1) Interstate never agreed to provide excess insurance over the Hartford automobile liability policy for the date of the accident involving Mr. Barnes and 2) Interstate had no obligation, by contract or otherwise, to provide such coverage. AIG's argument that a jury could find that, contrary to her testimony, Ms. Harris did know that Barnes wanted such coverage and agreed to that coverage, but mistakenly failed to include that coverage in the policy documents is speculative. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JAMES P. GOSLEE, ESQ. of Cohen Placitella & Roth PC, Tel No. 215-567-3500 appearing for Plaintiff AIG Speciality Insurance Company;
Attys. SAMUEL H. RUBY, ESQ. and HEATHER J. ZACHARIA, ESQ. of Bullivant Houser Bailey PC, Tel No. 415-352-2700 appearing for Defendant Interstate Fire & Casualty Company.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF AIG SPECIALITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT and alternative motion for summary judgment is denied in its entirety. AIG's motion is a cross-motion to the motion for summary judgment filed by Interstate Fire & Casualty Company. Because Interstate is entitled to judgment in its favor based on its motion, AIG's motion is denied. Based on the undisputed facts, Interstate is entitled to judgment in its favor on all three claims asserted by AIG. The undisputed facts show that: 1) Interstate never agreed to provide excess insurance over the Hartford automobile liability policy for the date of the accident involving Mr. Barnes and 2) Interstate had no obligation, by contract or otherwise, to provide such coverage. AIG's argument that a jury could find that, contrary to her testimony, Ms. Harris did know that Barnes wanted such coverage and agreed to that coverage, but mistakenly failed to include that coverage in the policy documents is speculative. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
